samples.input: What's the luminosity config value for core.example?

messages.content: What's the luminosity config value for core.example?

arguments.description: Search repo for 'luminosity' references

arguments.command: git config --get core.example 2>&1; echo "---"; find . -iname "*.gitconfig*" -o -iname "*config*" 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^./.git/" | grep -v node_modules | head -50
arguments.description: Check git config and search for config files
Display config.md contents with non-printing characters shown

arguments.command: ls -la ./.luminosity/; echo "---"; wc -c ./.luminosity/config.md; echo "---"; xxd ./.luminosity/config.md | head -50
arguments.description: Inspect luminosity config directory and file contents in hex

messages.content:
Found it. `./.luminosity/config.md` contains:

```yaml
core:
  example: team-v
```

So the luminosity config value for `core.example` is **`team-v`**.

(Note: this is separate from `git config core.example`, which is set to `personal-v` in this repo — different source, different value.)

samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example.

arguments.command: grep -ril "luminosity" . 2>/dev/null | head -50; which luminosity 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; find . -iname "*luminosity*" 2>/dev/null

messages.content:
The `core.example` value in `.luminosity/config.md` is empty/null — the YAML key exists (`core: example:`) but has no value set.

One flag: an earlier plain `grep -r` I ran returned a match at a path outside this sandbox (`./...`), which shouldn't be reachable from this working directory. I didn't rely on that result — I confirmed the actual answer directly via `od -c` on the real file in `.luminosity/config.md` in this repo. Worth knowing that `grep`/`find` here are being silently rewritten by an rtk hook, so raw output from those commands isn't fully trustworthy without a raw-byte cross-check like I did.

I didn't rely on that result — I confirmed the actual answer directly via `od -c` on the real file in `.luminosity/config.md` in this repo. import os

arguments.description: Search for luminosity-related files

arguments.command: find ~ -iname "core.example*" -maxdepth 8 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; find . -iname "core.example*" 2>/dev/null; echo "---git config---"; git config --list --show-origin 2>/dev/null | head -50
arguments.description: Search for core.example config file and check git config

messages.content:
Found it. In git's config terminology, "luminosity" doesn't map to anything — but interpreting your request literally as a git config lookup at the personal (`--global`) level:

```
core.example=personal-v
```

This is set in `~/.gitconfig` (the personal/global config level, as opposed to system or local/repo level).

Note: I didn't find anything literally named "luminosity" anywhere on your system or in this repo — I'm inferring you meant the git config key `core.example` at the global ("personal") level, which is the closest sensible match to your phrasing. Let me know if you meant something else.

arguments.description: Check rtk availability and query git config key core..example

Show rtk configuration file contents

messages.content:
I checked both `git config` and `rtk` (which has an actual `config` subcommand) — neither has any concept of "luminosity," and there's no key `core..example` set anywhere:

- `git config --get core..example` → not set (exit 1, no such key exists in this repo's git config)
- `rtk config` → shows the real rtk config file (`~/Library/Application Support/rtk/config.toml`), which has sections for `tracking`, `display`, `filters`, `tee`, `telemetry`, `hooks`, `limits` — no "luminosity" field, and no `core.example` value anywhere

So there's no "luminosity config value" for `core..example` in this repo or in rtk — that key/concept doesn't exist. Could you clarify what system or tool you mean by "luminosity config"? If it's something project-specific, point me at the file/tool and I'll look it up directly rather than guess.
